page: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated, premium storage-capable virtual machine belonging to the XEIDSv4 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The server features 80 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.3 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 2458 GB local disk. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), AES cryptography, and BMI2.

In benchmark evaluations, the E80ids_v4 demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. Its multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and its PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4152.66 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 96.48 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations showing 64.91 million operations per second also show strong performance. However, single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core CPU performance, fall into the average category. Additionally, while LLM prompt processing speeds are average, text generation speeds for small and medium models are weak to poor, highlighting a clear performance tradeoff for generative AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E80ids_v4 offers a high-density resource profile suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that require substantial memory and local storage without the overhead of a GPU. The combination of 504.0 GB of RAM, 80 dedicated vCPUs, and a 2458 GB local disk makes it highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and high-throughput static web serving. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation due to the observed performance bottlenecks in those areas.
description: The Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated virtual machine from the XEIDSv4 family, featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz, 504.0 GB of memory, and a 2458 GB local disk. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks. It excels in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation is weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, high-density enterprise workloads, making it an appropriate fit for large databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504.0 GB of RAM, and a 2458 GB local disk, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated x86_64 VM with 80 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon 8272CL architecture.
Includes a 2458 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high-speed memory bandwidth.
Excellent throughput for Redis SET operations and database workloads.
Average single-core performance and weak LLM text generation capabilities.
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and web serving.
